correction.rationale: Use positive points distributed through the ankle, instep, midfoot, forefoot, and toes, with negative points in the gray background and around the rectangular artifact regions. SAM refinement is safer than a polygon because the toe separations and ankle contour are detailed, while the correct character-perspective side should be verified by a human.

judgments.evidence: Localized evidence is the gray-background wedge and block artifacts above the instep/ankle, plus the implausible internal boundary. The full-person chain also raises a character-left/right assignment conflict, so this should not pass deterministic QA.

observations.contour: The contour follows the outer foot and toe silhouette in places, but has implausible straight segments, large excursions into background, and an incorrect internal curved boundary across the ankle/upper foot.
observations.full_context: The foreground foot is the visible target candidate, but the pose and crossed-leg chain make character-perspective side assignment inconsistent with the proposed right_foot_base label; human side verification is warranted.
observations.mask: The mask covers most of the foot, but also contains a large background wedge above the instep and multiple rectangular/block-shaped background inclusions near the upper and right edges. It is not a clean single anatomical component.
observations.neighbor_overlap: The mask appears to extend beyond the foot into surrounding background and may include adjacent ankle/lower-leg area; no clothing is visible in this crop.
observations.overlay: Red overlay visibly spills into gray background above the foot and has stepped rectangular protrusions around the ankle and instep; the foot boundary is otherwise approximately covered.
observations.source_crop: The crop clearly shows one bare foot and ankle against a gray background. The visible foot extends from the ankle at the upper-left through the toes at the lower-right.
